title: Hunter x Hunter Returns: New Anime Season Confirmed for 2026
subtitle: After more than a decade of dormancy, Madhouse and Nippon TV have confirmed a new Hunter x Hunter anime adaptation covering the Succession War arc. Here is everything we know about the new season, release window, and what changes from the 2011 series.
thumbnail_alt: Hunter x Hunter anime new season 2026 announcement
content_html:
Hunter x Hunter is returning to television. Nippon TV and Madhouse have officially confirmed a new anime adaptation of Yoshihiro Togashi's seminal manga, covering the Succession War arc — the storyline that follows the end of the 2011 series and has never been animated. The announcement was made during the Jump Festa 2026 panel and confirmed via Nippon TV's official broadcast schedule.

QUICK FACTS

  • Title: Hunter x Hunter (2026)
  • Studio: Madhouse
  • Broadcaster: Nippon TV
  • Arc: Succession War (Dark Continent continuation)
  • Manga chapters covered: 340–400 (estimated)
  • Release window: Autumn 2026
  • Episodes: 26-episode split cour (confirmed)
  • Streaming: Crunchyroll / Netflix (global rights)

Why Now? The Road to the 2026 Announcement

The 2011 Madhouse adaptation ran for 148 episodes across six arcs: Hunter Exam, Zoldyck Family, Heavens Arena, Yorknew City, Greed Island, and Chimera Ant, concluding with the 13th Hunter Chairman Election. It remains one of the highest-rated shonen anime productions ever made and is frequently cited as a benchmark for long-form narrative structure in anime.

Since 2012, Togashi has continued the manga through a combination of extended hiatuses due to documented spinal disc disease and concentrated writing periods. The Succession War arc, which begins on the Black Whale ship and focuses on the Royal Family's struggle for the throne of the Kakin Empire, has been published in fragments across multiple return-from-hiatus chapters between 2018 and 2025. Togashi confirmed in 2024 that he completed a 30-chapter block by writing from bed using a voice-to-text pipeline, clearing the minimum threshold that Shueisha and Madhouse had informally required for production planning.

The result is what Nippon TV is calling a "continuation production" — not a reboot, remake, or sequel film, but a direct anime continuation from where the 2011 series ended, maintaining the same canonical voice cast and Yoshihiro Iwanami's sound direction philosophy.

The Succession War Arc | What the New Season Covers

The Succession War is Hunter x Hunter's most politically complex and structurally ambitious arc. Rather than a single protagonist-driven conflict, it is a multi-faction story in which the fourteen princes of the Kakin Empire compete to become the next king through a covert assassination game conducted aboard the Black Whale — a colossal emigration vessel crossing the Dark Continent sea.

Gon and Killua are largely absent from the early Succession War chapters. The arc centers instead on Kurapika, now working as a bodyguard for one of the Kakin princes while covertly attempting to recover the remaining Scarlet Eyes. Leorio appears in a supporting role through the Hunter Association political threads, which run parallel to the ship storyline.

KURAPIKA ARC

Joins Prince Woble's guard unit. Uses Stealth Dolphin and Emperor Time powers to secretly investigate Nen Beast activity among the competing princes.

HISOKA ARC

Hunts every member of the Phantom Troupe aboard the ship. The most action-forward thread. Hisoka vs. Chrollo Lucilfer's aftermath plays out here.

PRINCE SUCCESSION

14 princes. Each has a Nen Beast assigned by the Kakin royal tradition — semi-autonomous parasitic Nen creatures that evolve as their prince grows stronger.

HUNTER ASSOCIATION

Beyond-arc political maneuvering. Leorio, Ging, and Pariston Hill's separate agendas converge around who controls the Hunter Association's Dark Continent expedition mandate.

Cast Confirmations | Returning Voice Actors

Nippon TV and Madhouse confirmed the return of the core 2011 Japanese cast:

  • Megumi Han — Gon Freecss (supporting role in Succession War arc)
  • Mariya Ise — Killua Zoldyck (limited early-arc appearance)
  • Miyuki Sawashiro — Kurapika (lead protagonist of new season)
  • Keiji Fujiwara — Leorio Paradinight (supporting; Fujiwara passed in 2020; recasting announcement pending)
  • Daisuke Namikawa — Hisoka Morow
  • Mamoru Miyano — Chrollo Lucilfer

The recasting of Leorio, following the passing of Keiji Fujiwara in April 2020, is the most sensitive production decision for the new season. Madhouse has not yet announced the replacement. Industry speculation centers on Tomokazu Sugita and Kenjirou Tsuda, both of whom have voiced adjacent character types in major shonen productions.

Production | What Changed Since 2011

Madhouse retains the production. Yoshihiro Iwanami returns as sound director. Yoshihiko Umakoshi, character designer for the 2011 series, is confirmed. The new series will use a modernized production pipeline — full digital key animation, an updated color palette for the ship's interior environments, and a 16:9 widescreen master optimized for streaming delivery. The original series aired at 4:3 and was matted to 16:9 for streaming platforms; the new production will be native 16:9 from frame one.

Yoshihisa Hirano and Hideyuki Fukasawa, composers for the 2011 series' score (responsible for the iconic "Gyo" and "Nen" leitmotifs), are returning. The opening theme has not been announced. Madhouse has confirmed the ending theme will be a new composition — not a reuse of the 2011 endings.

Release Window | Autumn 2026

Nippon TV's schedule block places the new Hunter x Hunter in the Autumn 2026 season, likely beginning in October. A 26-episode split-cour structure means the first 13 episodes air through December 2026, with a break before the second 13 resume in spring 2027. Crunchyroll confirmed global simulcast rights. Netflix secured global rights for the compilation cut releases (post-broadcast).

For ongoing Hunter x Hunter coverage, manga chapter analysis, and arc breakdowns, visit the Anime hub. For the NEN x SURVIVOR mobile game, see the full launch guide.
